name: asyauth
documentation: Unified authentication library

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.constants.asyauthProtocol.SSL
documentation: SSL/TLS schannel later

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INSUFFICIENT_MEMORY
documentation: Not enough memory is available to complete this request.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INVALID_HANDLE
documentation: The handle specified is invalid.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.UNSUPPORTED_FUNCTION
documentation: The function requested is not supported.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.TARGET_UNKNOWN
documentation: The specified target is unknown or unreachable.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INTERNAL_ERROR
documentation: The Local Security Authority (LSA) cannot be contacted.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.SECPKG_NOT_FOUND
documentation: The requested security package does not exist.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NOT_OWNER
documentation: The caller is not the owner of the desired credentials.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CANNOT_INSTALL
documentation: The security package failed to initialize and cannot be installed.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INVALID_TOKEN
documentation: The token supplied to the function is invalid.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CANNOT_PACK
documentation: The security package is not able to marshal the logon buffer, so the logon attempt has failed.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.QOP_NOT_SUPPORTED
documentation: The per-message quality of protection is not supported by the security package.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_IMPERSONATION
documentation: The security context does not allow impersonation of the client.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.LOGON_DENIED
documentation: The logon attempt failed.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.UNKNOWN_CREDENTIALS
documentation: The credentials supplied to the package were not recognized.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_CREDENTIALS
documentation: No credentials are available in the security package.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.MESSAGE_ALTERED
documentation: The message or signature supplied for verification has been altered.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.OUT_OF_SEQUENCE
documentation: The message supplied for verification is out of sequence.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_AUTHENTICATING_AUTHORITY
documentation: No authority could be contacted for authentication.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.BAD_PKGID
documentation: The requested security package does not exist.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CONTEXT_EXPIRED
documentation: The context has expired and can no longer be used.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INCOMPLETE_MESSAGE
documentation: The supplied message is incomplete. The signature was not verified.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INCOMPLETE_CREDENTIALS
documentation: The credentials supplied were not complete and could not be verified. The context could not be initialized.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.BUFFER_TOO_SMALL
documentation: The buffers supplied to a function was too small.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.WRONG_PRINCIPAL
documentation: The target principal name is incorrect.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.TIME_SKEW
documentation: The clocks on the client and server machines are skewed.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.UNTRUSTED_ROOT
documentation: The certificate chain was issued by an authority that is not trusted.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.ILLEGAL_MESSAGE
documentation: The message received was unexpected or badly formatted.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CERT_UNKNOWN
documentation: An unknown error occurred while processing the certificate.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CERT_EXPIRED
documentation: The received certificate has expired.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.ENCRYPT_FAILURE
documentation: The specified data could not be encrypted.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.DECRYPT_FAILURE
documentation: The specified data could not be decrypted.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.ALGORITHM_MISMATCH
documentation: The client and server cannot communicate because they do not possess a common algorithm.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.SECURITY_QOS_FAILED
documentation: The security context could not be established due to a failure in the requested quality of service (for example, mutual authentication or delegation).

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.UNFINISHED_CONTEXT_DELETED
documentation: A security context was deleted before the context was completed. This is considered a logon failure.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_TGT_REPLY
documentation: The client is trying to negotiate a context and the server requires user-to-user but did not send a ticket granting ticket (TGT) reply.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_IP_ADDRESSES
documentation: Unable to accomplish the requested task because the local machine does not have an IP addresses.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.WRONG_CREDENTIAL_HANDLE
documentation: The supplied credential handle does not match the credential associated with the security context.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CRYPTO_SYSTEM_INVALID
documentation: The cryptographic system or checksum function is invalid because a required function is unavailable.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.MAX_REFERRALS_EXCEEDED
documentation: The number of maximum ticket referrals has been exceeded.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.MUST_BE_KDC
documentation: The local machine must be a Kerberos domain controller (KDC), and it is not.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.STRONG_CRYPTO_NOT_SUPPORTED
documentation: The other end of the security negotiation requires strong cryptographics, but it is not supported on the local machine.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.TOO_MANY_PRINCIPALS
documentation: The KDC reply contained more than one principal name.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_PA_DATA
documentation: Expected to find PA data for a hint of what etype to use, but it was not found.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.PKINIT_NAME_MISMATCH
documentation: The client certificate does not contain a valid user principal name (UPN), or does not match the client name in the logon request. Contact your administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.SMARTCARD_LOGON_REQUIRED
documentation: Smart card logon is required and was not used.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.SHUTDOWN_IN_PROGRESS
documentation: A system shutdown is in progress.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.KDC_INVALID_REQUEST
documentation: An invalid request was sent to the KDC.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.KDC_UNABLE_TO_REFER
documentation: The KDC was unable to generate a referral for the service requested.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.KDC_UNKNOWN_ETYPE
documentation: The encryption type requested is not supported by the KDC.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.UNSUPPORTED_PREAUTH
documentation: An unsupported pre-authentication mechanism was presented to the Kerberos package.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.DELEGATION_REQUIRED
documentation: The requested operation cannot be completed. The computer must be trusted for delegation, and the current user account must be configured to allow delegation.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.BAD_BINDINGS
documentation: Client's supplied Security Support Provider Interface (SSPI) channel bindings were incorrect.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.MULTIPLE_ACCOUNTS
documentation: The received certificate was mapped to multiple accounts.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_KERB_KEY
documentation: No Kerberos key was found.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CERT_WRONG_USAGE
documentation: The certificate is not valid for the requested usage.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.DOWNGRADE_DETECTED
documentation: The system detected a possible attempt to compromise security. Ensure that you can contact the server that authenticated you.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.SMARTCARD_CERT_REVOKED
documentation: The smart card certificate used for authentication has been revoked. Contact your system administrator. The event log might contain additional information.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.ISSUING_CA_UNTRUSTED
documentation: An untrusted certification authority (CA) was detected while processing the smart card certificate used for authentication. Contact your system administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.REVOCATION_OFFLINE_C
documentation: The revocation status of the smart card certificate used for authentication could not be determined. Contact your system administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.PKINIT_CLIENT_FAILURE
documentation: The smart card certificate used for authentication was not trusted. Contact your system administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.SMARTCARD_CERT_EXPIRED
documentation: The smart card certificate used for authentication has expired. Contact your system administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.NO_S4U_PROT_SUPPORT
documentation: The Kerberos subsystem encountered an error. A service for user protocol requests was made against a domain controller that does not support services for users.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.CROSSREALM_DELEGATION_FAILURE
documentation: An attempt was made by this server to make a Kerberos-constrained delegation request for a target outside the server's realm. This is not supported and indicates a misconfiguration on this server's allowed-to-delegate-to list. Contact your administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.REVOCATION_OFFLINE_KDC
documentation: The revocation status of the domain controller certificate used for smart card authentication could not be determined. The system event log contains additional information. Contact your system administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.ISSUING_CA_UNTRUSTED_KDC
documentation: An untrusted CA was detected while processing the domain controller certificate used for authentication. The system event log contains additional information. Contact your system administrator.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.KDC_CERT_EXPIRED
documentation: The domain controller certificate used for smart card logon has expired. Contact your system administrator with the contents of your system event log.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.KDC_CERT_REVOKED
documentation: The domain controller certificate used for smart card logon has been revoked. Contact your system administrator with the contents of your system event log.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.INVALID_PARAMETER
documentation: One or more of the parameters passed to the function were invalid.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.DELEGATION_POLICY
documentation: The client policy does not allow credential delegation to the target server.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SEC_E.POLICY_NLTM_ONLY
documentation: The client policy does not allow credential delegation to the target server with NLTM only authentication.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_CRED.AUTOLOGON_RESTRICTED
documentation: The security does not use default logon credentials or credentials from Credential Manager.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_CRED.BOTH
documentation: Validate an incoming credential or use a local credential to prepare an outgoing token. This flag enables both other flags. This flag is not valid with the Digest and Schannel SSPs.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_CRED.INBOUND
documentation: Validate an incoming server credential. Inbound credentials might be validated by using an authenticating authority when InitializeSecurityContext (General) or AcceptSecurityContext (General) is called. If such an authority is not available, the function will fail and return SEC_E_NO_AUTHENTICATING_AUTHORITY. Validation is package specific.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_CRED.OUTBOUND
documentation: Allow a local client credential to prepare an outgoing token.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_CRED.PROCESS_POLICY_ONLY
documentation: The function processes server policy and returns SEC_E_NO_CREDENTIALS, indicating that the application should prompt for credentials.

classes.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_ATTRMASK
documentation: The buffer contains a bitmask for a SECBUFFER_READONLY_WITH_CHECKSUM buffer.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_EMPTY
documentation: This is a placeholder in the buffer array. The caller can supply several such entries in the array, and the security package can return information in them. For more information, see SSPI Context Semantics.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_DATA
documentation: The buffer contains common data. The security package can read and write this data, for example, to encrypt some or all of it.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_TOKEN
documentation: The buffer contains the security token portion of the message. This is read-only for input parameters or read/write for output parameters.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_PKG_PARAMS
documentation: These are transport-to-package–specific parameters. For example, the NetWare redirector may supply the server object identifier, while DCE RPC can supply an association UUID, and so on.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_MISSING
documentation: The security package uses this value to indicate the number of missing bytes in a particular message. The pvBuffer member is ignored in this type.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_EXTRA
documentation: The security package uses this value to indicate the number of extra or unprocessed bytes in a message.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_STREAM_TRAILER
documentation: The buffer contains a protocol-specific trailer for a particular record. It is not usually of interest to callers.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_STREAM_HEADER
documentation: The buffer contains a protocol-specific header for a particular record. It is not usually of interest to callers.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_MECHLIST
documentation: The buffer contains a protocol-specific list of object identifiers (OIDs). It is not usually of interest to callers.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_MECHLIST_SIGNATURE
documentation: The buffer contains a signature of a SECBUFFER_MECHLIST buffer. It is not usually of interest to callers.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_TARGET
documentation: This flag is reserved. Do not use it.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_CHANNEL_BINDINGS
documentation: The buffer contains channel binding information.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_CHANGE_PASS_RESPONSE
documentation: The buffer contains a DOMAIN_PASSWORD_INFORMATION structure.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_TARGET_HOST
documentation: The buffer specifies the service principal name (SPN) of the target.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_ALERT
documentation: The buffer contains an alert message.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_APPLICATION_PROTOCOLS
documentation: The buffer contains a list of application protocol IDs, one list per application protocol negotiation extension type to be enabled.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_SRTP_PROTECTION_PROFILES
documentation: The buffer contains the list of SRTP protection profiles, in descending order of preference.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_SRTP_MASTER_KEY_IDENTIFIER
documentation: The buffer contains the SRTP master key identifier.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_TOKEN_BINDING
documentation: The buffer contains the supported token binding protocol version and key parameters, in descending order of preference.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_PRESHARED_KEY
documentation: The buffer contains the preshared key. The maximum allowed PSK buffer size is 256 bytes.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_PRESHARED_KEY_IDENTITY
documentation: The buffer contains the preshared key identity.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECBUFFER_TYPE.SECBUFFER_DTLS_MTU
documentation:
In addition, BufferType can combine the following flags with any of the flags in the preceding table by using a bitwise-OR operation.
Value   Meaning
SECBUFFER_READONLY
2147483648 (0x80000000)
        The buffer is read-only with no checksum. This flag is intended for sending header information to the security package for computing the checksum. The package can read this buffer, but cannot modify it.
SECBUFFER_READONLY_WITH_CHECKSUM
268435456 (0x10000000)
        The buffer is read-only with a checksum
The buffer contains the setting for the maximum transmission unit (MTU) size for DTLS only. The default value is 1096 and the valid configurable range is between 200 and 64*1024.
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/desktop/api/sspi/ns-sspi-_secbuffer

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.C_ACCESS_TOKEN
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_AccessToken structure that specifies the access token for the current security context. This attribute is supported only on the server.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.C_FULL_ACCESS_TOKEN
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_AccessToken structure that specifies the access token for the current security context. This attribute is supported only on the server.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.CERT_TRUST_STATUS
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a CERT_TRUST_STATUS structure that specifies trust information about the certificate.This attribute is supported only on the client.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.CREDS
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_ClientCreds structure that specifies client credentials. The client credentials can be either user name and password or user name and smart card PIN. This attribute is supported only on the server.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.CREDS_2
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_ClientCreds structure that specifies client credentials. If the client credential is user name and password, the buffer is a packed KERB_INTERACTIVE_LOGON structure. If the client credential is user name and smart card PIN, the buffer is a packed KERB_CERTIFICATE_LOGON structure. If the client credential is an online identity credential, the buffer is a marshaled SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY_EX2 structure. This attribute is supported only on the CredSSP server. Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7, Windows Server 2008, Windows Vista, Windows Server 2003 and Windows XP:  This value is not supported.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.NEGOTIATION_PACKAGE
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_PackageInfo structure that specifies the name of the authentication package negotiated by the Microsoft Negotiate provider.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.PACKAGE_INFO
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_PackageInfostructure.Returns information on the SSP in use.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.SERVER_AUTH_FLAGS
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_Flags structure that specifies information about the flags in the current security context. This attribute is supported only on the client.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.SIZES
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_Sizes structure. Queries the sizes of the structures used in the per-message functions and authentication exchanges.

class_variables.name: asyauth.common.winapi.constants.SECPKG_ATTR.SUBJECT_SECURITY_ATTRIBUTES
documentation: The pBuffer parameter contains a pointer to a SecPkgContext_SubjectAttributes structure. This value returns information about the security attributes for the connection. This value is supported only on the CredSSP server. Windows Server 2008, Windows Vista, Windows Server 2003 and Windows XP:  This value is not supported.

classes.name: asyauth.common.winapi.functiondefs.SecBuffer
documentation:
Stores a memory buffer: size, type-flag, and POINTER. 
The type can be empty (0) or token (2).
InitializeSecurityContext will write to the buffer that is flagged "token"
and update the size, or else fail 0x80090321=SEC_E_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL.

classes.name: asyauth.common.winapi.functiondefs.SecBufferDesc
documentation:
Descriptor stores SECBUFFER_VERSION=0, number of buffers (e.g. one),
and POINTER to an array of SecBuffer structs.

classes.name: asyauth.protocols.spnegoex.protocol.messages.BYTE_VECTOR
documentation:
BYTE_VECTOR encapsulates a variable length array of octets (or bytes)
that are stored consecutively.  Each element in is a byte (8 bits).

classes.name: asyauth.protocols.spnegoex.protocol.messages.AUTH_SCHEME_VECTOR
documentation:
AUTH_SCHEME_VECTOR encapsulates a variable length array of
AUTH_SCHEMEs that are stored consecutively.  Each element is a
structure of the type AUTH_SCHEME.

classes.name: asyauth.protocols.spnegoex.protocol.messages.EXTENSION_VECTOR
documentation:
EXTENSION_VECTOR encapsulates a variable length array of EXTENSIONs
that are stored consecutively.  Each element is a structure of the
type EXTENSION.

classes.name: asyauth.protocols.spnegoex.protocol.messages.EXTENSION
documentation: This part is not tested, as the RFC doesn't define any extensions at the moment!

documentation: Not possible to perform this function due to unknown keys during relaying

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.relay.native.NTLMRelayHandler.modify_negotiate
documentation:
By default we don't modify the structures because there can be all kinds of integrity protections.
However, if you find some cool stuff then you can just populate the modify_negotiate_cb in the settings, 
but you MUST return both ntlmNegotiate and ntlmNegotiate_raw structures and/or indicate an error 
which must be of class Exception!

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.relay.native.NTLMRelayHandler.modify_challenge
documentation:
By default we don't modify the structures because there can be all kinds of integrity protections.
However, if you find some cool stuff then you can just populate the modify_authenticate_cb in the settings, 
but you MUST return both ntlmChallenge_server and ntlmChallenge_server_raw structures and/or indicate an error 
which must be of class Exception!

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.relay.native.NTLMRelayHandler.modify_authenticate
documentation:
By default we don't modify the structures because there can be all kinds of integrity protections.
However, if you find some cool stuff then you can just populate the modify_authenticate_cb in the settings, 
but you MUST return both ntlmAuthenticate and ntlmAuthenticate_raw structures and/or indicate an error 
which must be of class Exception!

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.relay.native.NTLMRelayHandler.authenticate_relay_server
documentation: This function is to be called by the server side which we obtain the auth material from

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.relay.native.NTLMRelayHandler.authenticate
documentation:
FRONT TOWARD ENEMY
This function is to be called by the client side.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvEOL
documentation: Indicates that this is the last AV_PAIR in the list. AvLen MUST be 0. This type of information MUST be present in the AV pair list.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvNbComputerName
documentation: The server's NetBIOS computer name. The name MUST be in Unicode, and is not null-terminated. This type of information MUST be present in the AV_pair list.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvNbDomainName
documentation: The server's NetBIOS domain name. The name MUST be in Unicode, and is not null-terminated. This type of information MUST be present in the AV_pair list.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvDnsComputerName
documentation: The fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the computer. The name MUST be in Unicode, and is not null-terminated.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvDnsDomainName
documentation: The FQDN of the domain. The name MUST be in Unicode, and is not null-terminated.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvDnsTreeName
documentation: The FQDN of the forest. The name MUST be in Unicode, and is not null-terminated.<13>

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvFlags
documentation: A 32-bit value indicating server or client configuration.

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvTimestamp
documentation: A FILETIME structure ([MS-DTYP] section 2.3.3) in little-endian byte order that contains the server local time. This structure is always sent in the CHALLENGE_MESSAGE.<16>

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvSingleHost
documentation: A Single_Host_Data (section 2.2.2.2) structure. The Value field contains a platform-specific blob, as well as a MachineID created at computer startup to identify the calling machine.<17>

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvAvTargetName
documentation: The SPN of the target server. The name MUST be in Unicode and is not null-terminated.<18>

class_variables.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPAIRType.MsvChannelBindings
documentation: A channel bindings hash. The Value field contains an MD5 hash ([RFC4121] section 4.1.1.2) of a gss_channel_bindings_struct ([RFC2744] section 3.11). An all-zero value of the hash is used to indicate absence of channel bindings.<19>

classes.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.structures.avpair.AVPairs
documentation: AVPairs is a dictionary-like object that stores the "AVPair list" in a key -value format where key is an AVPAIRType object and value is the corresponding object defined by the MSDN documentation. Usually it's string but can be other object as well
???? https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa374793(v=vs.85).aspx

The result is 24 bytes in length.

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.ntlm.creds_calc.netlm.verify
documentation:
Verifies the authentication data against the user credentials
Be careful! If the credtype is 'hash' then LM hash is expected!
\:param creds: dictionary containing the domain, user, hash/password
\:param credtype: can be 'plain' or 'hash' this indicates what type of credential lookup to perform
\:return: bool

documentation:
Returns the initial sequence number. It is 0 by default, but can be adjusted during authentication, 
by passing the 'seq_number' parameter in the 'authenticate' function

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.kerberos.client.sspi.KerberosClientSSPI.get_seq_number
documentation:
Fetches the starting sequence number. This is either zero or can be found in the authenticator field of the 
AP_REQ structure. As windows uses a random seq number AND a subkey as well, we can't obtain it by decrypting the 
AP_REQ structure. Insead under the hood we perform an encryption operation via EncryptMessage API which will 
yield the start sequence number

classes.name: asyauth.protocols.spnego.client.native.SPNEGOClientNative

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.spnego.client.native.SPNEGOClientNative.list_original_conexts
documentation: Returns a list of authentication context names available to the SPNEGO authentication.

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.spnego.client.native.SPNEGOClientNative.get_original_context
documentation:
Returns a copy of the original (not used) authentication context specified by name.
You may use this ctx to perform future authentication, as it has the user credentials

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.spnego.client.native.SPNEGOClientNative.add_auth_context
documentation:
Add an authentication context to the given authentication context name.
Valid names are:
        'NTLMSSP - Microsoft NTLM Security Support Provider'
        'MS KRB5 - Microsoft Kerberos 5'
        'KRB5 - Kerberos 5'
        'KRB5 - Kerberos 5 - User to User'
        'NEGOEX - SPNEGO Extended Negotiation Security Mechanism'
        
Context MUST be already set up!

methods.name: asyauth.protocols.spnego.client.native.SPNEGOClientNative.authenticate
documentation:
This function is called (multiple times) during negotiation phase of a protocol to determine hich auth mechanism to be used
Token is a byte array that is an ASN1 NegotiationToken structure.
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/openspecs/windows_protocols/ms-spng/d4f2b41c-5f9e-4e11-98d0-ade76467095d
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/openspecs/windows_protocols/ms-spng/94ccc4f8-d224-495f-8d31-4f58d1af598e
# SPNEGO has been assigned the following object identifier (OID): so.org.dod.internet.security.mechanism.snego (1.3.6.1.5.5.2)
